content.problemStatement:
Consider the following algorithm, which generates a (not necessarily uniformly) random permutation of numbers 111 through NNN:

`P := [1, 2, ..., N]
for i in 1..N do
    j := rand(1, N)
    swap(P[i], P[j])
`Here, `rand(1, N)` returns a uniformly random integer between 111 and NNN inclusive. Let's denote the probability that the permutation generated by this algorithm is PPP by p(P)p(P)p(P).

Find a permutation P1P_1P1​ such that p(P1)p(P_1)p(P1​) is maximum possible and a permutation P2P_2P2​ such that p(P2)p(P_2)p(P2​) is minimum possible.
content.inputFormat: The first and only line of the input contains a single integer NNN.
content.outputFormat:
Print two lines. The first line should contain NNN space-separated integers denoting your permutation P1P_1P1​. The second line should contain NNN space-separated integers denoting your permutation P2P_2P2​.

If there are multiple answers, you may print any one.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
There are two possible permutations, [1,2][1, 2][1,2] and [2,1][2, 1][2,1]. Both are equally likely to appear as the result of the given algorithm, so any pair of permutations is a valid answer.
